type_:StreamXFernStreamingUnionStreamReq.docs: A discriminated union request matching the Vectara pattern (FER-9556). Each variant inherits stream_response from UnionStreamRequestBase via allOf. The importer pins stream_response to Literal[True/False] at this union level, but the allOf inheritance re-introduces it as boolean in each variant, causing the type conflict.

type_:StreamXFernStreamingUnionRequest.docs: A discriminated union request matching the Vectara pattern (FER-9556). Each variant inherits stream_response from UnionStreamRequestBase via allOf. The importer pins stream_response to Literal[True/False] at this union level, but the allOf inheritance re-introduces it as boolean in each variant, causing the type conflict.

properties.name: event
properties.docs: An event type field inside the data payload that collides with the SSE envelope's event field used for discrimination.

properties.name: query
properties.docs: The prompt or query to complete.

properties.name: query
properties.docs: The prompt or query to complete.

properties.name: stream
properties.docs: Whether to stream the response. This field is nullable (OAS 3.1 type array), which previously caused the const literal to be overwritten by the nullable type during spread in the importer.

type_:CompletionFullResponse.docs: Full response returned when streaming is disabled.

properties.name: tokens
properties.docs: Number of tokens in this chunk.

type_:CompletionStreamChunk.docs: A single chunk in a streamed completion response.

type_:UnionStreamRequestBase.docs: Base schema for union stream requests. Contains the stream_response field that is inherited by all oneOf variants via allOf. This schema is also referenced directly by a non-streaming endpoint to ensure it is not excluded from the context.

type_:UnionStreamMessageVariant.docs: A user input message. Inherits stream_response from base via allOf.

type_:UnionStreamInterruptVariant.docs: Cancels the current operation. Inherits stream_response from base.

type_:UnionStreamCompactVariant.docs: Requests compaction of history. Inherits stream_response from base and adds compact-specific fields.

type_:UnionStreamRequest.docs: A discriminated union request matching the Vectara pattern (FER-9556). Each variant inherits stream_response from UnionStreamRequestBase via allOf. The importer pins stream_response to Literal[True/False] at this union level, but the allOf inheritance re-introduces it as boolean in each variant, causing the type conflict.

endpoints.name: streamProtocolNoCollision
endpoints.displayName: Protocol context with no field collision and mixed data types

value.docs: SSE stream with protocol-level discrimination and mixed data types

endpoints.docs: Uses discriminator with mapping, x-fern-discriminator-context set to protocol. Because the discriminant is at the protocol level, the data field can be any type or absent entirely. Demonstrates heartbeat (no data), string literal, number literal, and object data payloads.

endpoints.name: streamProtocolCollision
endpoints.displayName: Protocol context with event field collision and mixed data types

value.docs: SSE stream with protocol context and event field collision

endpoints.docs: Same as endpoint 1, but the object data payload contains its own "event" property, which collides with the SSE envelope's "event" discriminator field. Tests whether generators correctly separate the protocol-level discriminant from the data-level field when context=protocol is specified.

endpoints.name: streamDataContext
endpoints.displayName: Explicit data context with discriminant flattened via allOf

value.docs: SSE stream with discriminator context set to data

endpoints.docs: x-fern-discriminator-context is explicitly set to "data" (the default value). Each variant uses allOf to extend a payload schema and adds the "event" discriminant property at the same level. There is no "data" wrapper. The discriminant and payload fields coexist in a single flat object. This matches the real-world pattern used by customers with context=data.

endpoints.name: streamNoContext
endpoints.displayName: No context extension, defaults to data context

value.docs: SSE stream with no discriminator context hint

endpoints.docs: The x-fern-discriminator-context extension is omitted entirely. Tests whether Fern correctly infers the default behavior (context=data) when the extension is absent. Same flat allOf pattern as endpoint 3.

endpoints.name: streamProtocolWithFlatSchema
endpoints.displayName: Protocol context with flat allOf schema pattern

value.docs: SSE stream with protocol context but flat allOf schemas

endpoints.docs: Mismatched combination: context=protocol with the flat allOf schema pattern that is normally used with context=data. Shows what happens when the discriminant is declared as protocol-level but the schema uses allOf to flatten the event field alongside payload fields instead of wrapping them in a data field.

endpoints.name: streamDataContextWithEnvelopeSchema
endpoints.displayName: Data context with envelope+data schema pattern

value.docs: SSE stream with data context but envelope+data schemas

endpoints.docs: Mismatched combination: context=data with the envelope+data schema pattern that is normally used with context=protocol. Shows what happens when the discriminant is declared as data-level but the schema separates the event field and data field into an envelope structure.

endpoints.name: streamOasSpecNative
endpoints.displayName: OAS 3.2 spec-native SSE pattern with inline variants and contentSchema

value.docs: SSE stream following the OAS 3.2 spec example pattern

endpoints.docs: Follows the pattern from the OAS 3.2 specification's own SSE example. The itemSchema extends a base Event schema via $ref and uses inline oneOf variants with const on the event field to distinguish event types. Data fields use contentSchema/contentMediaType for structured payloads. No discriminator object is used. Event type resolution relies on const matching.

endpoints.name: streamXFernStreamingCondition_stream
endpoints.displayName: x-fern-streaming with stream-condition and $ref request body

properties.name: query
properties.docs: The prompt or query to complete.

endpoints.docs: Uses x-fern-streaming extension with stream-condition to split into streaming and non-streaming variants based on a request body field. The request body is a $ref to a named schema. The response and response-stream point to different schemas.

endpoints.name: streamXFernStreamingCondition
endpoints.displayName: x-fern-streaming with stream-condition and $ref request body

properties.name: query
properties.docs: The prompt or query to complete.

endpoints.docs: Uses x-fern-streaming extension with stream-condition to split into streaming and non-streaming variants based on a request body field. The request body is a $ref to a named schema. The response and response-stream point to different schemas.

endpoints.name: streamXFernStreamingSharedSchema_stream
endpoints.docs: Uses x-fern-streaming with stream-condition. The request body $ref (SharedCompletionRequest) is also referenced by a separate non-streaming endpoint (/validate-completion). This tests that the shared request schema is not excluded from the context during streaming processing.

endpoints.name: streamXFernStreamingSharedSchema
endpoints.docs: Uses x-fern-streaming with stream-condition. The request body $ref (SharedCompletionRequest) is also referenced by a separate non-streaming endpoint (/validate-completion). This tests that the shared request schema is not excluded from the context during streaming processing.

endpoints.name: validateCompletion
endpoints.displayName: Non-streaming endpoint sharing request schema with endpoint 10
endpoints.docs: A non-streaming endpoint that references the same SharedCompletionRequest schema as endpoint 10. Ensures the shared $ref schema remains available and is not excluded during the streaming endpoint's processing.

endpoints.name: streamXFernStreamingUnion_stream
endpoints.displayName: x-fern-streaming with discriminated union request body

requestBody.docs: A discriminated union request matching the Vectara pattern (FER-9556). Each variant inherits stream_response from UnionStreamRequestBase via allOf. The importer pins stream_response to Literal[True/False] at this union level, but the allOf inheritance re-introduces it as boolean in each variant, causing the type conflict.

value.docs: A discriminated union request matching the Vectara pattern (FER-9556). Each variant inherits stream_response from UnionStreamRequestBase via allOf. The importer pins stream_response to Literal[True/False] at this union level, but the allOf inheritance re-introduces it as boolean in each variant, causing the type conflict.

endpoints.docs: Uses x-fern-streaming with stream-condition where the request body is a discriminated union (oneOf) whose variants inherit the stream condition field (stream_response) from a shared base schema via allOf. Tests that the stream condition property is not duplicated in the generated output when the base schema is expanded into each variant.

endpoints.name: streamXFernStreamingUnion
endpoints.displayName: x-fern-streaming with discriminated union request body

requestBody.docs: A discriminated union request matching the Vectara pattern (FER-9556). Each variant inherits stream_response from UnionStreamRequestBase via allOf. The importer pins stream_response to Literal[True/False] at this union level, but the allOf inheritance re-introduces it as boolean in each variant, causing the type conflict.

value.docs: A discriminated union request matching the Vectara pattern (FER-9556). Each variant inherits stream_response from UnionStreamRequestBase via allOf. The importer pins stream_response to Literal[True/False] at this union level, but the allOf inheritance re-introduces it as boolean in each variant, causing the type conflict.

endpoints.docs: Uses x-fern-streaming with stream-condition where the request body is a discriminated union (oneOf) whose variants inherit the stream condition field (stream_response) from a shared base schema via allOf. Tests that the stream condition property is not duplicated in the generated output when the base schema is expanded into each variant.

endpoints.name: validateUnionRequest
endpoints.displayName: Non-streaming endpoint referencing the union base schema
endpoints.docs: References UnionStreamRequestBase directly, ensuring the base schema cannot be excluded from the context. This endpoint exists to verify that shared base schemas used in discriminated union variants with stream-condition remain available.

endpoints.name: streamXFernStreamingNullableCondition_stream
endpoints.displayName: x-fern-streaming with nullable stream condition field

properties.name: query
properties.docs: The prompt or query to complete.

properties.name: stream
properties.docs: Whether to stream the response. This field is nullable (OAS 3.1 type array), which previously caused the const literal to be overwritten by the nullable type during spread in the importer.

endpoints.docs: Uses x-fern-streaming with stream-condition where the stream field is nullable (type: ["boolean", "null"] in OAS 3.1). Previously, the spread order in the importer caused the nullable type array to overwrite the const literal, producing stream?: true | null instead of stream: true. The const/type override must be spread after the original property.

endpoints.name: streamXFernStreamingNullableCondition
endpoints.displayName: x-fern-streaming with nullable stream condition field

properties.name: query
properties.docs: The prompt or query to complete.

properties.name: stream
properties.docs: Whether to stream the response. This field is nullable (OAS 3.1 type array), which previously caused the const literal to be overwritten by the nullable type during spread in the importer.

endpoints.docs: Uses x-fern-streaming with stream-condition where the stream field is nullable (type: ["boolean", "null"] in OAS 3.1). Previously, the spread order in the importer caused the nullable type array to overwrite the const literal, producing stream?: true | null instead of stream: true. The const/type override must be spread after the original property.

endpoints.name: streamXFernStreamingSseOnly
endpoints.displayName: x-fern-streaming with SSE format but no stream-condition
endpoints.docs: Uses x-fern-streaming with format: sse but no stream-condition. This represents a stream-only endpoint that always returns SSE. There is no non-streaming variant, and the response is always a stream of chunks.
